member.name: T:System.Base36
member.summary: Represents a base-36 numeric value, which uses the digits 0–9 and the letters A–Z to encode integers.
member.remarks:
The struct provides functionality for working with base-36 encoded numbers, including parsing, formatting, and arithmetic operations. It supports implicit and explicit conversions to and from integers and strings, and implements various interfaces for comparison, formatting, and parsing.
Base-36 encoding is case-insensitive, but this implementation always uses uppercase letters for formatting.
The range of values supported by corresponds to the range of a 32-bit signed integer ( to ).

member.name: F:System.Base36.Digits
member.summary: The characters used in base-36 representation, where '0' = 0, '1' = 1, ..., '9' = 9, 'A' = 10, ..., 'Z' = 35.

member.name: F:System.Base36.Zero
member.summary: Represents the number zero (0) in base-36.

member.name: F:System.Base36.One
member.summary: Represents the number one (1) in base-36.

member.name: F:System.Base36.MinValue
member.summary: Represents the smallest possible value of , which is equivalent to .

member.name: F:System.Base36.MaxValue
member.summary: Represents the largest possible value of , which is equivalent to .

member.name: P:System.Base36.Value
member.summary: The underlying integer value.

member.name: M:System.Base36.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a Base36 from an integer value.
member.param: The integer value.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Explicit(System.Base36)~System.Int32
member.summary: Converts the specified instance to its equivalent integer representation.
member.param: The instance to convert.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Implicit(System.String)~System.Base36
member.summary: Implicitly converts a base-36 string (e.g., "9IX", "ZZ") to Base36. Throws FormatException on invalid text and ArgumentNullException on null.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Implicit(System.Base36)~System.String
member.summary: Implicitly formats Base36 to its base-36 string (uppercase A–Z).

member.name: M:System.Base36.CompareTo(System.Base36)
member.summary: Compares the current instance with another object and returns an integer that indicates whether the current instance precedes, follows, or occurs in the same position in the sort order as the other object.
member.param: The object to compare to the current instance.
member.returns:
A signed integer that indicates the relative order of the objects being compared:
Less than zero: The current instance precedes in the sort order.
Zero: The current instance occurs in the same position in the sort order as .
Greater than zero: The current instance follows in the sort order.

member.name: M:System.Base36.System#IComparable#CompareTo(System.Object)
member.summary: Compares the current instance with another object and returns an integer that indicates whether the current instance precedes, follows, or occurs in the same position in the sort order as the other object.
member.param: The object to compare with the current instance. Must be of type .
member.returns:
A signed integer that indicates the relative order of the objects being compared:
Less than zero: The current instance precedes in the sort order.
Zero: The current instance occurs in the same position in the sort order as .
Greater than zero: The current instance follows in the sort order.
member.exception: Thrown if is not of type .

member.name: M:System.Base36.Equals(System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether the current instance is equal to the specified instance.
member.param: The instance to compare with the current instance.
member.returns: if the current instance and the specified instance have the same value; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current instance.
member.param: The object to compare with the current instance.
member.returns: if the specified object is a instance and is equal to the current instance; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns the hash code for the current object.
member.remarks: The hash code is derived from the property. This method is suitable for use in hashing algorithms and data structures such as a hash table.
member.returns: A 32-bit signed integer that serves as the hash code for the current object.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Equality(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether two instances are equal.
member.param: The first instance to compare.
The second instance to compare.
member.returns: if the values of and are equal; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Inequality(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether two instances are not equal.
member.param: The first instance to compare.
The second instance to compare.
member.returns: if the values of and are not equal; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_LessThan(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether one instance is less than another.
member.param: The first instance to compare.
The second instance to compare.
member.returns: if the value of is less than the value of ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_GreaterThan(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether the value of the left operand is greater than the value of the right operand.
member.param: The first operand to compare.
The second operand to compare.
member.returns: if the value of is greater than the value of ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_LessThanOrEqual(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether the value of the left instance is less than or equal to the value of the right instance.
member.param: The first instance to compare.
The second instance to compare.
member.returns: if the value of is less than or equal to the value of ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Determines whether the value of the left operand is greater than or equal to the value of the right operand.
member.param: The first operand to compare.
The second operand to compare.
member.returns: if the value of is greater than or equal to the value of ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Addition(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Adds two values and returns the result.
member.param: The first value to add.
The second value to add.
member.returns: A new instance representing the sum of and .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Subtraction(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Subtracts one value from another and returns the result.
member.param: The minuend, represented as a value.
The subtrahend, represented as a value.
member.returns: A new instance representing the result of the subtraction.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Multiply(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Multiplies two values and returns the result.
member.param: The first value to multiply.
The second value to multiply.
member.returns: A new instance representing the product of and .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Division(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Divides one value by another and returns the result as a new instance.
member.remarks: The division is performed on the underlying numeric values of the instances.
member.param: The dividend, represented as a instance.
The divisor, represented as a instance.
member.returns: A new instance representing the result of the division.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Modulus(System.Base36,System.Base36)
member.summary: Computes the remainder of the division of one value by another.
member.param: The dividend, represented as a value.
The divisor, represented as a value.
member.returns: A new instance representing the remainder of the division of by .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_UnaryPlus(System.Base36)
member.summary: Returns the specified instance unchanged.
member.param: The instance to return.
member.returns: The same instance as the input parameter .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_UnaryNegation(System.Base36)
member.summary: Negates the value of the specified instance.
member.param: The instance to negate.
member.returns: A new instance representing the negated value of .

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Increment(System.Base36)
member.summary: Increments the value of the specified instance by one.
member.param: The instance to increment.
member.returns: A new instance representing the incremented value.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Decrement(System.Base36)
member.summary: Decrements the value of the specified instance by one.
member.param: The instance to decrement.
member.returns: A new instance representing the decremented value.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Addition(System.Base36,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds an integer value to a instance and returns the resulting value.
member.param: The instance to add to.
The integer value to add to the instance.
member.returns: A new instance representing the sum of the original value and the integer.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Subtraction(System.Base36,System.Int32)
member.summary: Subtracts an integer value from a instance and returns the resulting value.
member.param: The instance to subtract from.
The integer value to subtract.
member.returns: A new instance representing the result of the subtraction.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Multiply(System.Base36,System.Int32)
member.summary: Multiplies the numeric value of a instance by an integer.
member.param: The instance to be multiplied.
The integer by which to multiply the value of .
member.returns: A new instance representing the product of the multiplication.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Division(System.Base36,System.Int32)
member.summary: Divides the value of the specified instance by an integer and returns the result as a new instance.
member.remarks: This operator performs integer division, truncating any fractional part of the result.
member.param: The instance to be divided.
The integer divisor. Must not be zero.
member.returns: A new instance representing the result of the division.

member.name: M:System.Base36.op_Modulus(System.Base36,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the remainder of the division of the value of the specified instance by an integer.
member.param: The instance whose value is to be divided.
The integer divisor. Must be a non-zero value.
member.returns: A new instance representing the remainder of the division.

member.name: M:System.Base36.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation of the current object.
member.remarks: This method uses the invariant culture for formatting. To customize the formatting, use the ToString() overload.
member.returns: A string that represents the current object.

member.name: M:System.Base36.ToString(System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Formats as base-36 by default. If format == "D" or "d", formats decimal (base-10). Any other format is treated as base-36.

member.name: M:System.Base36.TryFormat(System.Span{System.Char},System.Int32@,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Attempts to format the current value into the provided character span.
member.remarks: If the specified format is 'D' or 'd', the value is formatted as a decimal number. For other formats, the method may use an alternative representation, such as Base36. Ensure that the span is large enough to accommodate the formatted value; otherwise, the method will return .
member.param: The span of characters to which the formatted value will be written.
When this method returns, contains the number of characters written to .
A read-only span of characters that specifies the format to use. Use 'D' or 'd' for decimal formatting; other formats may be used for alternative representations.
An object that provides culture-specific formatting information.
member.returns: if the value was successfully formatted and written to ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.FormatBase36(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts the specified integer value to its equivalent string representation in base 36.
member.remarks: Base 36 is a numeral system that uses 36 distinct characters: the digits '0'-'9' and the letters 'A'-'Z'. This method handles both positive and negative integers, as well as zero.
member.param: The integer value to convert. Can be positive, negative, or zero.
member.returns: A string representing the base 36 equivalent of the specified integer. The string will use characters '0'-'9' and 'A'-'Z' for the digits, and a leading '-' for negative values.

member.name: M:System.Base36.TryFormatBase36(System.Int32,System.Span{System.Char},System.Int32@)
member.summary: Attempts to format the specified integer value as a Base-36 string and write it to the provided destination buffer.
member.remarks: Base-36 encoding uses the characters '0'-'9' and 'A'-'Z' to represent values. Negative numbers are prefixed with a '-' character. If the buffer is too small to contain the formatted result, the method returns and does not modify the buffer.
member.param: The integer value to format. Can be positive, negative, or zero.
The buffer to which the formatted Base-36 string will be written. The buffer must be large enough to hold the result.
When this method returns, contains the number of characters written to the buffer, if the operation succeeds; otherwise, 0.
member.returns: if the value was successfully formatted and written to the buffer; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.Parse(System.String,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Converts the specified string representation of a base-36 number to its equivalent.
member.param: The string representation of a base-36 number to convert.
An object that provides culture-specific formatting information. This parameter is currently not used.
member.returns: A instance that represents the number specified by .
member.exception: Thrown if is not a valid base-36 number or if the value is outside the range of a 32-bit signed integer.

member.name: M:System.Base36.TryParse(System.String,System.IFormatProvider,System.Base36@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse the specified string representation of a Base36 number into its equivalent value.
member.remarks: This method does not throw an exception if the conversion fails. Instead, it returns and sets to .
member.param: The string representation of the Base36 number to parse. Cannot be .
An that supplies culture-specific formatting information, or to use the current culture.
When this method returns, contains the value equivalent to the number contained in , if the conversion succeeded; otherwise, . This parameter is passed uninitialized.
member.returns: if was successfully parsed; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.Parse(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Converts the specified read-only span of characters representing a base-36 number into its equivalent.
member.param: The span of characters containing the base-36 number to convert.
An object that provides culture-specific formatting information. This parameter is currently ignored.
member.returns: A instance that represents the number specified by .
member.exception: Thrown if does not represent a valid base-36 number or if the value is outside the range of a 32-bit signed integer.

member.name: M:System.Base36.TryParse(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.IFormatProvider,System.Base36@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse the specified span of characters as a Base36 number.
member.remarks: The method supports optional leading '+' or '-' signs to indicate the number's sign. If the span contains invalid characters or the resulting value exceeds the range of a Base36 number, the method returns and sets to .
member.param: The span of characters to parse.
An object that provides culture-specific formatting information. This parameter is currently ignored.
When this method returns, contains the parsed value if the conversion succeeded, or if the conversion failed. This parameter is passed uninitialized.
member.returns: if the span was successfully parsed as a Base36 number; otherwise, .

member.name: M:System.Base36.CharToDigit(System.Char)
member.summary: Converts a character to its corresponding numeric value.
member.remarks: The conversion is case-insensitive. Non-alphanumeric characters will result in a return value of -1.
member.param: The character to convert. Can be a digit ('0'-'9') or an uppercase or lowercase letter ('A'-'Z' or 'a'-'z').
member.returns: The numeric value of the character: 0-9 for digits, 10-35 for letters ('A' or 'a' = 10, 'B' or 'b' = 11, ..., 'Z' or 'z' = 35). Returns -1 if the character is not a valid digit or letter.
